learning_outcomes.description: Explore and discuss Chinas economic, political and social development, drawing on relevant sources of information to analyse issues and inform thinking.
Research and present informed opinions about Chinas agriculture, resource management and environmental issues, and policies related to them.
Identify and discuss Chinas stance and public relations with reference to its economic, social, foreign and international connections and policies.
Review the internal and external trends that impact on China's labour market, and determine consequent impacts on poverty and inequality.
Reflect on the nature of doing business in China for the purpose of establishing strategies for engaging effectively with Chinese cultures and norms.

assessments.due_date: The Presentation file is to be submitted using Turnitin by 17 Aug 2026, 4pm.The Presentation will be held during your sign-on tutorial in week 4 to 13. Essay is due TWO weeks after the individual presentation.
assessments.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Essay/ Critique, Participation/ Student contribution, Performance, Practical/ Demonstration, Presentation, Reflection
assessments.task_description:
For this assessment item

  • Choose any topical or controversial issue that relates to China. Ideas on a range of issues will also be provided in the first lecture.
  • Find two articles that address this issue from different perspectives. 
  • Write an essay and deliver a presentation on the issue, the different perspectives in it, and draw your own conclusions on the issue.

Your structure for both should include:

  • AN INTRODUCTION that summarises the underlying issue so that the reader understands the situation and context.
  • A CRITICAL EVALUATION of the substance of your TWO chosen articles with regard to your chosen issue.
  • CONCLUSIONS in relation to your critical evaluation.

This review task is split into two parts:

TUTORIAL PRESENTATION (10% of course marks). During your first tutorial (Week 2) you will be allocated

(a) a week in the semester to present the key findings of your analysis, and

(b) the other 2 student presentations in which you will engage in the Q&A session.

Your presentation is a short summary of key points and should last for 5-7 minutes and must be delivered live and in-person during your sign-on tutorial ONLY. A video submission is not allowed. The presentation will be followed by class discussion, Q&A and tutor feedback, designed to help improve your essay. The presentation will be recorded.

FINAL ESSAY (20% of course marks). After your presentation you will have TWO weeks to make updates and improvements to your essay before submitting the final version via Turnitin. The essay should be a maximum of 2,000 words including diagrams, figures, and tables but excluding references.

Points to note:

  • Use the APA 7th referencing style ONLY. Please refer to About APA 7th - APA 7th referencing style - Library Guides at University of Queensland Library
  • You are expected to have more references than just the two articles you are reviewing. 
  • When you submit your final essay, please use the provided template (located in the course blackboard) for a cover page including your name, student ID, course code/name, essay title, and the number of words in your essay.
  • There are no other specific formatting requirements, but please ensure your essay is clearly structured, flows well and is professionally presented.
  • In tutorials, you are expected to make critical comments and pose questions of other students' presentations that you are assigned to.
assessments.submission_guidelines: There are four types of delivery required for this assessment:
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Reflections on key issues in lectures
assessments.task_description:
For this assessment item

  • At the end of each lecture throughout the semester you will be provided with a question/task that arise from the lecture material.
  • Students are required to write a short-written response (in pdf) to the question/ task and submit it the week after (Tue, 4pm) via Turnitin.
  • Responses must incorporate information provided in lectures, however further additional information and sources from outside lecture material can be used.
  • In responding to the set questions/tasks, your responses must demonstrate critical reflection on the underlying material.
  • Each response/ submission should be limited to less than 500 words (approximately one page), excluding references.
  • Only the best 8 out of 10 submissions will be counted towards the final grade.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
The final assessment item for this course is an exam

  • The exam will be invigilated in-person held on St. Lucia Campus.
  • It will cover the major course materials and concepts addressed this semester. 
  • It will consist of a variety of short answer and short essay questions.
  • Scheduling details will be managed by UQ Examinations, and you will be advised of the exam timing once this is finalised. 
  • Further details about the exam will be discussed in class and practice questions will be provided.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
